stories.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy concerned as U.S. suspends Russia sanctions
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.

stories.title: CNN correspondent shares his takeaways from his recent visit to Iran
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.

stories.title: All 6 U.S. crew are dead after a plane goes down in Iraq, as Mideast war toll mounts
stories.description: The U.S. military confirmed that all six crew members on an KC-135 aircraft died after the refueling plane went down in western Iraq, raising the U.S. death toll after two weeks of war with Iran.

stories.title: Exiled Iranians watch the war from afar, wondering what awaits when it ends
stories.description: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.

stories.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
stories.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.

stories.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
stories.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Iranians react to new supreme leader's first address
stories.description: People in Iran are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV.

stories.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
stories.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: 'Follow the smoke': BBC spends day with emergency teams as Israel strikes south Lebanon
stories.description: The BBC visited the city of Nabatieh, witnessing the pace and scale of Israel's bombing as its war with Hezbollah continues to escalate.

stories.title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
stories.description: The echoes between the conflicts are certainly there but there are also profound differences.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
stories.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
stories.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
stories.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

stories.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
stories.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
stories.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

stories.title: Explosions reported on two foreign vessels in the Gulf, authorities say
stories.description: Pressure has intensified on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping lanes, since Israel and the US attacked Iran last month.

stories.title: Explosion shakes Tehran amid state-organised rally after Israeli warning
stories.description: A blast rocked a major pro-Palestinian rally in Tehran on Friday after Israel warned it could target the area, as the nearly two-week war between Israel and Iran escalated across the Middle East. Washington ordered roughly 2,500 additional Marines to the region, bolstering its mi...

stories.title: Trump’s Iran Strategy: Former US State Department official Jennifer Gavito speaks to France 24
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Jennifer Gavito, former US diplomat and Senior Advisor at The Cohen Group, about Washington’s strategy in the current conflict and what President Donald Trump is seeking to achieve. They discuss the objectives behind the US military campaign, the...

stories.title: Iran: A 'Nepo-Ayatollah', the 'Hubris trap" & the costs of a "very complete war"
stories.description: It’s been a week that’s seen a new Ayatollah proclaimed in Iran. Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei, has not been seen in public and is believed to be in hiding after reportedly being injured in the same strike that killed his father. State tele...

stories.title: How will Russia benefit from US decision to ease oil sanctions?
stories.description: The United States has eased some restrictions on Russian oil, letting shipments already at sea continue until early April in the aim of calming global markets amid Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine and European leaders criticised the move, saying it undermined peac...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: A sailor stranded in the Gulf fears for his safety
stories.description: Since the beginning of the conflict in the Middle East, many merchant vessels have been stranded in the Gulf, as the Strait of Hormuz has become a high-risk area following several attacks attributed to Iran. Among them is Wang Shang, a Chinese sailor aboard a cargo ship, who desc...

stories.title: ‘We cut down the menu’: Cooking gas crisis hits restaurants in India amid war in Iran
stories.description: Restaurants in India are suffering the consequences of disruptions to supplies of cooking gas caused by the war in the Middle East. Two restaurant owners describe the mounting challenges of operating under the crisis and the measures they’re taking to adapt.

stories.title: Zelensky in Paris: Macron vows to keep support for Ukraine
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron Friday vowed to keep up pressure on Russia to end its four-year invasion of Ukraine after Washington partially rolled back sanctions against Moscow to cool oil prices sent soaring by the Middle East war. FRANCE 24's Camille Knight reports.

stories.title: Six US service members killed in plane crash over Iraq
stories.description: ll six crew members aboard a US military refueling aircraft that ​crashed in western Iraq are confirmed to have been killed, the US military said on Friday. The deaths add to the seven US service members who have already been killed as part ‌of US operations against Iran which be...

stories.title: Iran attacks target Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Dubai
stories.description: New waves of attacks struck Gulf nations on Friday after Iran renewed its threats on oil facilities. Tehran has unleashed waves of drone and missile strikes against neighbouring states hosting US military assets, including Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain and Dubai. Hoda Abdel-Hamid r...

stories.title: Iran-made drone killed French soldier in Iraqi Kurdistan
stories.description: An Iranian drone has killed a French soldier in Iraq's autonomous Kurdistan region, his commanding officer said on Friday, after President Emmanuel Macron confirmed the first French military death in the Middle East war. Since US-Israeli strikes on Iran last month engulfed the Mi...

stories.title: Zelensky in Paris: Macron pledges no 'respite' for Russia despite Mideast war
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron Friday vowed to keep up pressure on Russia to end its four-year invasion of Ukraine after Washington partially rolled back sanctions against Moscow to cool oil prices sent soaring by the Middle East war. Kyiv's allies worry that the United States ...

stories.title: Israel strikes bridge over Litani River in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military said it carried out an air strike on ​a bridge in southern Lebanon on Friday, in what appeared to be the first time in the current campaign against Hezbollah that it acknowledged attacking civilian infrastructure. The military said the Zrariyeh bridge, which ...

stories.title: Global energy crisis: Asia feels the squeeze
stories.description: Asian nations, including India, are feeling the impact of the ongoing war in Iran. We explore how dependent the region is on West Asia for energy and consider whether New Delhi should position itself as a mediator to help bring the conflict to an end.

stories.title: US media coverage of the war in Iran: Fact, fiction and propaganda
stories.description: Polls in the United States suggest the war in Iran is unusually unpopular compared with past US military conflicts. There has been propaganda, video-game footage and facts spun into fiction, but also excellent reporting and crucial analysis of the innocent lives lost. This week, ...

stories.title: Worshippers pray outside Al-Aqsa amid closure on al-Quds Day
stories.description: Muslim worshippers prayed outside Jerusalem’s Al-Aqsa compound as Israeli authorities kept holy sites closed.

stories.title: Iranian missile strike in central Israel sets building ablaze
stories.description: Smoke and flames rising beside a road in central Israel after an Iranian missile attack, the Israeli army said.

stories.title: Qatar’s interior minister says security situation ‘stable’ amid Iran war
stories.description: Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad says Qatar will 'not hesitate' to ensure its stability as US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: What rats do’: US says Iranian leaders hiding as leaders appear in Tehran
stories.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Iran’s leadership was “desperate and hiding,” after US-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Success uncertain, but Israelis continue to back ‘heroic’ war with Iran
stories.description: Israeli public overwhelmingly supports war with Iran, rallying behind Netanyahu despite political divisions.

stories.title: Ukraine, EU allies slam US decision to roll back Russia oil sanctions
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says move 'does not help peace' as Europe reels from soaring energy prices linked to Iran war.

stories.title: Ukraine finds new role as protector of US, Gulf allies amid Iran war
stories.description: Having abandoned Ukraine, the US has sought Kyiv's support in defence from Iranian drones.

stories.title: Thousands march worldwide in solidarity with Palestine, Iran on al-Quds Day
stories.description: Despite war, al-Quds Day marches in Tehran saw thousands protest, voicing anger against Israel and the US.

stories.title: Air attack hits as Iran’s chief justice gives lives interview at rally
stories.description: An air attack hit in Tehran as Iran’s chief justice gave lives interview at an al-Quds rally

stories.title: US’s Hegseth claims new Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured
stories.description: Pete Hegseth's remarks come a day after Khamenei pledged to keep fighting in first statement since he was named leader.

stories.title: US’s new scramble for Africa is biomedical imperialism
stories.description: From Zimbabwe to Zambia, US health deals are raising fears of a new era of biomedical extractivism.

stories.title: Pentagon elevates investigation into Iran school strike
stories.description: The US military said on Friday it has elevated the investigation into a devastating February 28 strike on an Iranian girls’ school after media reports revealed the probe shows US forces were likely responsible. Iran has said the strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh School killed 168 c...

stories.title: Deadly blast rocks Iran as leaders rally in show of defiance
stories.description: Deadly explosions rocked Tehran close to a pro-government rally attended by top officials on Friday, as Israel and Iran unleashed fresh strikes in a war that has ignited the Middle East and threatens to torpedo the world economy. Since erupting on February 28 with US-Israeli atta...

stories.title: Breakdown of diplomacy further complicates Iran nuclear issue, Pakistan warns Security Council
stories.description: Pakistan told the UN Security Council on Thursday that the breakdown of diplomacy in the Middle East has further complicated the Iranian nuclear issue, as Secretary-General António Guterres warned that the escalating crisis poses a “grave threat to international peace and securit...

stories.title: Assailant dead after ramming vehicle into Michigan synagogue
stories.description: A 41-year-old man was killed on Thursday after ramming his pickup truck into a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, causing a blaze and triggering a huge police response. US media reported the assailant had recently lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon,...

stories.title: Oil market becomes hostage to Iran war
stories.description: TEHRAN: Rescue workers gather as residents collect their belongings from the rubble of a residential building, damaged by US-Israeli strikes.—AFP • IMO to weigh Hormuz threats at extraordinary session as US Navy ‘not ready to escort ships’• Iran denies mining strait, offers passa...

stories.title: Strike on Beirut kills 12 after Israel’s ‘land grab’ threat
stories.description: SMOKE rises after Israeli air strikes target buildings in Beirut’s central Bachoura neighbourhood, close to the headquarters of the Lebanese government.—Reuters • 28 hurt in seafront attack targeting civilians, displaced families; death toll in Lebanon near 700• Hezbollah retalia...

stories.title: Rahul sees ‘Epstein link’ in Modi’s capitulation
stories.description: NEW DELHI: India has been blowing hot and cold at Iran over the oil flow disruption through the Hormuz and its targeting of US assets in Gulf states, but it is also talking to Tehran to allow its oil to be shipped, reports said on Thursday. The apparent capitulation of Prime Mini...

stories.title: Gunman dead after attack on Michigan synagogue
stories.description: An unidentified shooter was killed on Thursday after exchanging gunfire with security in an attack on a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, police said. Police said the gunman was dead following the attack on the Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield. Law enforc...

stories.title: In first remarks, Iran’s new supreme leader vows to avenge martyrs, keep Strait of Hormuz closed
stories.description: Iran will ​avenge the blood of its martyrs, keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and attack US bases, new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba ‌Khamenei said on Thursday in a statement read out on state television, his first remarks since succeeding his father following his assassination...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
